title: CurvatureIntegral
summary: Specializes the finite-part framework to renormalized integrals of curvature invariants.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes the finite-part framework to renormalized integrals of curvature invariants.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/PE/Renormalization/CurvatureIntegral.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Curvature Integral Cutoff Data. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: structure CurvatureIntegralCutoffData (X : PESpace.{u, v}) {Invariant : Type z} (I : Invariant)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: curvature Integral Cutoff Functional. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def curvatureIntegralCutoffFunctional (X : PESpace.{u, v}) {Invariant : Type z} (I : Invariant) : CutoffFunctional (CurvatureIntegralCutoffData.{u, v, w, z} X I) Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Renormalized Curvature Integral. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Curvature Integral Anomaly. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def CurvatureIntegralAnomaly {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Selects or identifies the constant term in a Laurent/log cutoff expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral_eq_finitePart {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : RenormalizedCurvatureIntegral D = finitePart D.cutoffExpansion

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Selects or identifies the logarithmic coefficient in a cutoff expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem CurvatureIntegralAnomaly_eq_logAnomaly {X : PESpace} {Invariant : Type z} {I : Invariant} (D : CurvatureIntegralCutoffData X I) : CurvatureIntegralAnomaly D = logAnomaly D.cutoffExpansion

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
